description: Replace zero-trade baseline acceptance criterion with multi-objective fitness that penalizes inaction. Implement Pareto front tracking for strategies with trades > MIN_TRADES, accepting non-dominated solutions by (Sharpe, win_rate, trade_count). Initialize LLM with best trading strategy (Sharpe -4.44, 407 trades, 44% win rate) rather than zero-trade baseline. Add constraint: reject any strategy with trades < MIN_TRADES to force exploration of trading solutions. Replace greedy best-only criterion with elite population that maintains diverse trading strategies.

Implement a robust halt mechanism that strictly enforces hard halts based on research document directives. This involves ensuring the ODIN execution loop reads and acts upon halt commands before initiating any optimization or strategy execution. The current failure mode, where halts are ignored, must be addressed at a fundamental level.

Revert all LOKI changes after Gen 2126 and restore the Gen 2126 incumbent configuration. Freeze further code modifications until the halt checklist is complete. Implement a mechanism to ensure the research program document (including halt conditions) is read at session startup and visible to ODIN. Fix the deduplication cache to prevent repeated evaluation of identical configurations. Ensure MAX_TRADES enforcement is functional.

Implement a checksum verification mechanism to ensure ODIN reads the research document. This is a critical structural change to address the core issue of ODIN not processing the document's findings. Additionally, the logic for accepting incumbent strategies needs to be re-evaluated to prevent the acceptance of degraded performance (e.g., lower Sharpe with significantly more trades) and to ensure the correct, high-performing Gen 2126 configuration is restored and maintained as the benchmark. The deduplication logic also needs to be robustly implemented to prevent identical configurations from being evaluated multiple times without rejection.
